alerts.customer_profile: SME FX broker, declared monthly volume ~GBP 80k

factors.detail: 9 transfers each just under the GBP 10,000 reporting threshold within 72h
inbound GBP 84k moved out across the 9 transfers within 36h of arrival
3 of 4 counterparties added in the last 5 days

explanation.headline: Potential structuring activity: rapid movement of large inbound funds via multiple sub-threshold transfers to newly added counterparties
why_it_fired: The alert combines four independent risk signals: (1) nine consecutive outbound transfers each just under the GBP 10,000 reporting threshold within a 72-hour window - a classic structuring pattern;
(2) rapid onward movement of an inbound GBP 84k within 36 hours, suggesting minimal hold or review;

(3) three of four destination counterparties added to the customer's approved list within the last 5 days, indicating new and unvetted relationships; and (4) the total 72-hour outflow (~GBP 85k) matches the inbound amount and exceeds the customer's declared typical monthly volume, compressing normal activity into a very short timeframe.
what_to_check: Verify whether the 9 transfers and the inbound receipt are genuinely linked (same underlying transaction or invoice) or independent activity
Review the business rationale for each transfer: are they consistent with the customer's declared FX broker activity?
Check the identity, location, and business relationship of the three newly added counterparties - verify KYC documentation and any existing sanctions/adverse media
Examine the source of the inbound GBP 84k: who sent it, from which country/entity, and under what stated purpose
Confirm whether the customer has a legitimate explanation for why activity of this scale and velocity deviates from their profile and declared monthly volume
Review historical patterns for the customer: is this a one-off spike or part of an emerging pattern?

alerts.customer_profile: Retail account, salaried, avg balance GBP 3k

factors.detail: forwarding wallet resolves to a high-risk jurisdiction VASP
no activity for 14 months, then GBP 22k in/out in one day

explanation.headline: Dormant retail account suddenly receives and rapidly forwards substantial funds to high-risk jurisdiction
explanation.typology: Potential money laundering / account takeover / layering scheme
why_it_fired: Three contributing factors combined to generate a 60/100 risk score: (1) Account showed no activity for 14 months then processed GBP 22,000 inbound and GBP 21,500 outbound in a single day, inconsistent with stated retail/salaried profile;
(2) 98% of received funds were forwarded within 4 hours, indicating rapid transit rather than genuine account use;
(3) Destination wallet resolves to a VASP in a high-risk jurisdiction, elevating the risk profile of the fund transfer.
what_to_check: Verify the source of the inbound GBP 22,000 (legitimate salary/expected transfer vs. third-party movement); confirm customer authorization for the rapid outbound transfer; review the nature and prior relationship (if any) with the receiving wallet and its jurisdiction; examine account access logs for signs of compromise or unusual login patterns; assess whether the customer profile (salaried, GBP 3k typical balance) aligns with sudden activity of this magnitude; check whether the receiving wallet is flagged for sanctions, fraud, or other financial crime concerns.

factors.detail: counterparty 'Rahim Yusufov' matches OFAC-style list entry at 0.86 similarity
single inbound 5x the customer's typical transaction size

explanation.headline: Sanctions name match and unusually large inbound transfer flagged for review
explanation.typology: Potential sanctions evasion; structuring or unusual activity
why_it_fired: Two rules contributed to the alert: (1) The counterparty name 'Rahim Yusufov' achieved 0.86 fuzzy-match similarity to an entry on an OFAC-style sanctions list (+40 points);
(2) The inbound transfer of GBP 15,000 is approximately 5× the customer's typical transaction size (+15 points).
Combined score of 55/100 triggered REVIEW disposition.
what_to_check: Verify the identity and legitimacy of counterparty 'Rahim Yusufov' against current OFAC and other relevant sanctions lists
Confirm whether the customer has a plausible business reason for this inbound transfer (e.g., client payment, project milestone, consulting engagement)
Review the customer's stated occupation (freelance consultant) and transaction history to determine if GBP 15,000 aligns with expected contract values
Examine any prior transactions with this counterparty or similar names
Check for any customer self-identification or documentation that explains the source of funds

alerts.customer_profile: Freight company, steady GBP 200k/month across EU corridors

explanation.headline: Low-risk alert: routine payments with single new supplier
why_it_fired: Delta Logistics GmbH made a payment to a first-time Italian supplier within the last 7 days.
The transaction was invoice-matched, indicating documented business justification.
No velocity or threshold anomalies were detected; payments remain consistent with the customer's established pattern of €200k monthly across EU corridors.
what_to_check: Verify the invoice documentation supporting the Italian counterparty payment
Confirm the Italian supplier's business registration and legitimacy
Review whether this supplier aligns with Delta Logistics' freight operations (e.g., port services, customs, logistics partners)
Check if similar suppliers have been onboarded previously in this corridor
